author: Waiz, R. M.
title: Masihi Git ki Kitab
notes: Christian hymnal in Urdu. End of URDU—POETRY (serials 37–58 this quarter).

author: Ghulam Mustafa, Hafiz
title: Hindustan aur Angrezi Hakumat
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer condemns the anarchical outrages perpetrated in India and compares the British rule with the governments of foreign countries, old and new, and trying to prove the superiority of the former.
publisher: The author who is Subedar, Regiment No. 92 Punjabis
notes: Loyalist tract against 'anarchical outrages' (revolutionary violence) by a serving Indian Army subedar; 100 free copies. Rare POLITICS section — direct window on surveillance-era political print.

author: Haji Rahm Shahid
title: Tarifha-i-Government-i-Aliya
gloss: A pamphlet describing the blessings of British rule in India.
notes: 'Praises of the Exalted Government' — loyalist panegyric.

title: Ulil Amr
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to show that loyalty to Government, whether Muhammadan or not, is enjoined by the Quran, with a note on the blessings of the British rule.
publisher: Compiled and published by Qazi Ghulam Muhi-ud-din, Akhgar, Editor "Al-Aziz"
notes: Quranic argument (ulu'l-amr) for loyalty to non-Muslim government, by a newspaper editor — theological loyalism after 1909 reforms. All three POLITICS entries this quarter are pro-government.

author: Abdul Basit, Hafiz, alias Sayad Muhammad Alam
title: Risala Musamma ba Qaul-ul-Fasl
gloss: A controversial pamphlet in which the writer, who is a Shia, tries to prove that a certain verse of the Quran which is held by the Sunnis to be in praise of the first Caliph does not establish his [first Caliph's] excellence over others.
notes: URDU—RELIGION serials continue from Q2. Dual date: 1328 Hijri / July 1910. Shia–Sunni polemic.

flags.issue: margin serial partially cut; read as 53 (54, 55 follow; page 44 resumes at 56)

author: Abdul Haji, Maulana
title: Silah-i-Rihm
gloss: A booklet in which the writer discusses from the Muhammadan point of view, quoting the Quran and traditions, the importance and sanctity of respecting relations on the mother's side.

author: Ahmad Khan, Sir, Sayad
title: Al-Khutubat-ul-Ahmadiya
gloss: A collection of lectures by Sir Sayad Ahmad Khan on the early Arabs and on Islam, refuting objections against the faith.
notes: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan's Khutbat-i-Ahmadiya (reply to Muir's Life of Mahomet), 752 pp. — Vakil Trading Co. canon-reprint program continues.
